_comment: (site 86_US95&SH8), the FUSION_PLAN.md §6 acceptance set for ROADMAP Item 42.
Members are raw [sensor, oid] keys (sensor = oid % 10). Each group was verified
by trajectory inspection (junction kinematics + a no-confusable-track-nearby
must come out as exactly one fused track with exactly these members; 'single'
entries are controls that must NOT merge with anything (a stationary object,
and two distinct simultaneous vehicles queuing nose-to-tail). Slots 4/5 are
the sensors' secondary (cls=0) streams; they carry ~17 ft of systematic offset
because the per-sensor calibration rightly refuses their sparse pair sets.

labels.note: NE-bound through the S0/S1 overlap; 7.6 s dual coverage, dedup
arrives on the diagonal, stops ~60 s at the light at (530,227), resumes; S1->S0 handoff mid-acceleration with 5.6 s overlap

NE-through truck; 421531 is S1's double-track twin dying 11 ft from 421491's endpoint at the same moment. Corrected 2026-07-13: 421505 was mislabeled into this group - the seam evidence (2-point 421501 born+dying at 716510's death spot as 421505 starts) pins it to the platooning partner below, which the 2026-07-10 inspection could not see

the NE-through platooner: first tracked as 715470, stops ~72 s at the light (drops at (403,516), resumes 12.8 ft away as 716510 on the same heading), then hands to the vendor-combined slot-5 id via the instant 2-point S1 acquisition 421501 (2026-07-13 observation round)

SW-bound into the stop bar. Corrected 2026-07-13: 420685 was labeled onto 715090 (queued ~15 ft alongside), but the seam is unambiguous - 715380 dies 6.4 ft and the 1-point 420681 2.0 ft from 420685's birth, 0.1 s before
creeps to a stop at (632,-927), drops 3.9 s, resumes 26 ft on; isolated corridor, no confusable track
stationary object at (345,563) tracked 3.5 min by S0 only; nothing may merge into it
queue arrival; distinct from and concurrent with 422971 stopped one car-length away
the neighbouring queued vehicle of 422921; the pair pins two-nearby-vehicles-stay-separate on real data
